title: Quick Fungal DNA Extraction from Colonies on Plates
author: Johannes Debler
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bwprpdm6

steps.title: %s %s 1M TrisHCl pH 8
steps.instruction: %s %s 1M TrisHCl pH 8.0 100 ml 12.14 g Tris base or Trizma base up to 100 ml deionised H2O Adjust pH concentrated HCl %s %s 2M KCl (Potassium chloride) 200 ml 15.11 g KCl (Potassium chloride) up to 200 ml deionised H2O A B 1 500 mM NaEDTA (pH 8.0) 100 ml 2 18.6 g EDTA disodium salt dihydrate 3 up to 100 ml deionised H 2 O Adjust pH to 8.0, otherwise the EDTA won't go into solution 500 mM NaEDTA 100 ml 18.6 g EDTA disodium salt dihydrate up to 100 ml deionised H2O Autoclave stock solutions at 121°C

steps.title: %s %s Extraction Buffer 100 ml...
steps.instruction: %s %s Extraction Buffer 100 ml 2M KCl 50 ml 1M Tris-HCl pH 8.0 10 ml 500 mM NaEDTA 2 ml deionised H2O up to 100 ml

steps.title: Per sample prepare 2 x 1
steps.instruction: Per sample prepare 2 x 1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes . Label them with sample number and add: Tube 1: 500 ul Extraction Buffer + 1 stainless steel bead Tube 2: 300 ul Isopropanol

steps.title: With a sterile toothpick or pipette...
steps.instruction: With a sterile toothpick or pipette tip remove a fungal colony or a piece of mycelia of about 3-5 mm x 3-5 mm in size from a plate and put it into Tube 1 (containing the Extraction Buffer and steel bead). Try not to use a piece that is too big, as that might lead to carryover of too many PCR inhibitors. I usually use my toothpick like a sewing machine needle, punching out a piece of mycelium all the way to the bottom of the agar plate. Then I use the toothpick to slice underneath the actual mycelial mat to try to cut off as much of the agar as possible.

steps.title: Homogenise samples for 5 min at...
steps.instruction: Homogenise samples for 5 min at 1500 rpm in the MiniG 1600. (SPEX, SP 1600, MiniG 1600 Automated Tissue Homogenizer and Cell Lyser, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/5myft4w.jpg )

steps.title: Pellet cell debris by centrifugation at 17
steps.instruction: Pellet cell debris by centrifugation at 17.000 x g for 5 min .

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to Tube 2 (Containing...
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to Tube 2 (Containing 300 ul Isopropanol) Vortex for 5 - 10 seconds Pellet DNA by centrifugation for 5 min at 17.000 x g

steps.title: Discard supernatant Add 800 ul 70%...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant Add 800 ul 70% Ethanol Vortex mix sample, make sure pellet comes loose from bottom of the tube Pellet DNA by centrifugation for 5 min at 17.000 x g

steps.title: Discard supernatant Remove as much of...
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant Remove as much of the ethanol as possible by drawing drops out with a pipette tip Let leftover ethanol evaporate for 5-10 min Dissolve pellet in 50-100 ul H 2 O or 1x TE buffer Measure DNA concentration (e.g. Nanodrop) store DNA at -20°C
